SEARCH
Package tage.nodeControllers

Class BobbingController

java.lang.Object
tage.NodeController
tage.nodeControllers.BobbingController
public class BobbingController extends NodeController
BobbingController causes target GameObjects to bob up and down using a sine wave on local Y. This controller is used as the custom NodeController for A2.

The controller caches each target's initial local translation (its base position) the first time it is applied, then continuously offsets the local Y component around that base. Multiple targets are supported by tracking base position, time, and a phase offset per GameObject.

Author:
Givin Yang, Nicole Joshua Espinoza

  • Constructor Details

    • BobbingController

      public BobbingController(Engine e, float amplitudeUnits, float periodSeconds)
      Creates a bobbing controller with the specified amplitude and period.

      Targets move along their local Y axis using a sine wave with the given period. The first time a target is processed, its current local translation is stored as the base position the bobbing will oscillate around.

  • Method Details

    • apply

      public void apply(GameObject go)
      Applies the bobbing animation to the specified GameObject.

      This method is called by the engine/controller system. It uses NodeController.getElapsedTime() to advance a per-object timer (in seconds), then computes a sine-wave Y offset using the configured period and amplitude. The resulting translation is written back to the target via GameObject.setLocalTranslation(Matrix4f).

      Specified by:
      apply in class NodeController

    • rebase

      public void rebase(GameObject go)
      Resets the stored base translation for a target so bobbing continues around its new position. This is useful after manual transforms such as placing a photo on the wall.
